title: Cosmos Tic-Tac-Toe — outwit a perfect minimax CPU
summary: A real, self-contained tic-tac-toe game against the computer in one HTML file with zero dependencies. Click any cell (or use keys 1-9 in numpad layout) to place your glowing X; a CPU running a full minimax search responds with the perfect O, so the best you can force is a draw. Winning lines light up with an aurora glow, a scoreboard tallies your wins, draws and CPU wins, and R or the button restarts the round. It ships with a deterministic self-playing attract demo for capture: a full game plays out for both sides and ends in a highlighted win or draw, then resets.
usage_notes: RUN: double-click index.html. CONTROLS: click/tap a cell to place X, or press keys 1-9 mapped in numpad layout (7/8/9 top row, 1/2/3 bottom row); the CPU answers automatically. GOAL: get three in a row before the minimax CPU does — it plays optimally so a draw is a good result; the HUD scoreboard tracks YOU / DRAW / CPU and the winning line glows; press R or Restart to play again. KEY TECHNIQUES: the CPU is a true recursive minimax over the game tree; the AUTO attract demo is fully deterministic — mulberry32 seeds a scripted X player, a winning seed is chosen at load, and autoState(ms) replays the move list as a pure function of time so window.__seek(t) reproduces the exact board, the just-placed mark's pop animation, the win line and the scoreboard from t alone. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 self-plays + window.__seek(t). TUNE: MOVE_MS per-move pace, HOLD_MS finished-board hold, T0OFF (frame-0 offset), seed search in pickGame.
